title: VimFu — What Is the Command Line?
description: Pressing : opens the command line at the bottom of the screen. This is where you type Ex commands to control Vim.

setup.comment: Set TERM for proper color support in Neovim

setup.content:
# Greeting App

def hello(name):
    return f"Hello, {name}!"

def goodbye(name):
    return f"Goodbye, {name}!"

print(hello("world"))
print(goodbye("world"))

steps.say: In Vim, pressing colon opens the command line at the bottom of the screen. This is where you type Ex commands.
Watch the bottom of the screen. Press colon.
See the colon at the bottom? Vim is waiting for a command. Press Escape to cancel.
Try again. Colon, then type set number, then Enter.
Line numbers appeared. The command executed.
Colon opens the command line. Type a command and press Enter. This is the gateway to hundreds of powerful Ex commands.
